subject: Product Management
title: Saying No to Customers
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Saying No to Customers.

hook.text: A paying customer demands a feature. If you build it, you distort the product. If you refuse, you might lose them.

buildup.text: The temptation to build for one loud customer is enormous — especially when revenue is at stake.

discovery.text: Henry Ford's (possibly apocryphal) quote: 'If I had asked people what they wanted, they would have said faster horses.'

twist.text: The right response: understand the problem behind the request. 'You want export to PDF' might mean 'I need to share reports.'

climax.text: Solving the underlying problem instead of the stated request often produces a better solution that serves more customers.

punchline.text: Listen to the problem, not the solution. Then solve it your way.

quiz.question: How should PMs handle specific customer feature demands?
options: Understand the underlying problem behind the request and solve it in a way that serves more customers
Build exactly what the customer asks for
Ignore all customer feedback to maintain product vision
